symptoms.name: headache
symptoms.description: Pain or pressure in the head

symptoms.name: migraine aura
symptoms.description: Visual disturbances preceding headache

symptoms.name: dizziness
symptoms.description: Feeling lightheaded or unsteady

symptoms.name: blurred vision
symptoms.description: Difficulty focusing or seeing clearly

symptoms.name: eye pain
symptoms.description: Pain in or around the eyes

symptoms.name: ear pain
symptoms.description: Pain in the ear canal or middle ear

symptoms.name: hearing loss
symptoms.description: Partial or complete inability to hear

symptoms.name: sore throat
symptoms.description: Pain or irritation in the throat

symptoms.name: nasal congestion
symptoms.description: Blocked or stuffy nose

symptoms.name: runny nose
symptoms.description: Excessive nasal discharge

symptoms.name: sneezing
symptoms.description: Involuntary expulsion of air from nose

symptoms.name: cough
symptoms.description: Repetitive expulsion of air from lungs

symptoms.name: chest pain
symptoms.description: Pain or discomfort in the chest area

symptoms.name: shortness of breath
symptoms.description: Difficulty breathing or feeling breathless

symptoms.name: wheezing
symptoms.description: High-pitched whistling sound during breathing

symptoms.name: heart palpitations
symptoms.description: Awareness of rapid or irregular heartbeat

symptoms.name: abdominal pain
symptoms.description: Pain in the stomach or belly area

symptoms.name: nausea
symptoms.description: Feeling of sickness with urge to vomit

symptoms.name: vomiting
symptoms.description: Forceful expulsion of stomach contents

symptoms.name: diarrhea
symptoms.description: Frequent loose or watery bowel movements

symptoms.name: constipation
symptoms.description: Infrequent or difficult bowel movements

symptoms.name: bloating
symptoms.description: Feeling of fullness or swelling in the abdomen

symptoms.name: back pain
symptoms.description: Pain in the upper or lower back

symptoms.name: muscle aches
symptoms.description: Diffuse pain in muscles throughout the body

symptoms.name: joint pain
symptoms.description: Pain or stiffness in one or more joints

symptoms.name: joint swelling
symptoms.description: Visible swelling around a joint

symptoms.name: numbness
symptoms.description: Loss of sensation in a body part

symptoms.name: tingling
symptoms.description: Pins-and-needles sensation

symptoms.name: fever
symptoms.description: Elevated body temperature above 38C / 100.4F

symptoms.name: chills
symptoms.description: Feeling of coldness often with shivering

symptoms.name: fatigue
symptoms.description: Extreme tiredness or lack of energy

symptoms.name: night sweats
symptoms.description: Excessive sweating during sleep

symptoms.name: weight loss
symptoms.description: Unintentional loss of body weight

symptoms.name: weight gain
symptoms.description: Unintentional increase in body weight

symptoms.name: loss of appetite
symptoms.description: Reduced desire to eat

symptoms.name: skin rash
symptoms.description: Change in skin color, texture or appearance

symptoms.name: itching
symptoms.description: Uncomfortable sensation causing desire to scratch

symptoms.name: hives
symptoms.description: Raised, itchy welts on the skin

symptoms.name: swollen lymph nodes
symptoms.description: Enlarged lymph nodes in neck, armpit or groin

symptoms.name: difficulty swallowing
symptoms.description: Trouble or pain when swallowing

symptoms.name: frequent urination
symptoms.description: Needing to urinate more often than usual

symptoms.name: painful urination
symptoms.description: Burning or pain during urination

symptoms.name: anxiety
symptoms.description: Persistent feelings of worry or nervousness

symptoms.name: insomnia
symptoms.description: Difficulty falling or staying asleep

symptoms.name: confusion
symptoms.description: Difficulty thinking clearly or concentrating

conditions.name: Common Cold
conditions.description: A viral infection of the upper respiratory tract causing mild symptoms that typically resolve within 7-10 days.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If symptoms last more than 10 days, fever exceeds 103F (39.4C), or symptoms worsen after initial improvement.

conditions.name: Influenza (Flu)
conditions.description: A contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses, often more severe than a cold with sudden onset.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you have difficulty breathing, persistent chest pain, confusion, or severe vomiting.

conditions.name: Migraine
conditions.description: A neurological condition causing intense, throbbing headache usually on one side, often accompanied by nausea and sensitivity to light.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If headache is sudden and severe (thunderclap), accompanied by fever, stiff neck, confusion, or neurological symptoms.

conditions.name: Allergic Rhinitis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the nasal passages caused by an allergic response to airborne substances like pollen or dust.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If symptoms significantly affect quality of life, or over-the-counter medications are not effective.

conditions.name: Gastroenteritis
conditions.description: Inflammation of the stomach and intestines, commonly called stomach flu, usually caused by viral or bacterial infection.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you cannot keep fluids down for 24 hours, have bloody diarrhea, fever over 104F, or signs of dehydration.

conditions.name: Urinary Tract Infection
conditions.description: An infection in any part of the urinary system, most commonly affecting the bladder and urethra.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you have fever, chills, back or side pain (possible kidney infection), blood in urine, or symptoms persist after treatment.

conditions.name: Asthma
conditions.description: A chronic condition in which the airways narrow, swell, and produce extra mucus, making breathing difficult.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you have rapid worsening of shortness of breath, no improvement after using inhaler, or shortness of breath with minimal activity.

conditions.name: Hypertension
conditions.description: Persistently elevated blood pressure that can damage blood vessels and organs over time. Often has no obvious symptoms.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If blood pressure readings consistently exceed 140/90, or you experience severe headache, chest pain, or vision changes.

conditions.name: Type 2 Diabetes
conditions.description: A chronic metabolic condition where the body becomes resistant to insulin or does not produce enough, leading to high blood sugar.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you experience extreme thirst, very frequent urination, unexplained weight loss, or blurred vision.

conditions.name: Tension Headache
conditions.description: The most common type of headache, typically causing a dull, aching pain on both sides of the head.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If headaches are frequent (more than 15 days per month), increasingly severe, or accompanied by neurological symptoms.

conditions.name: Pneumonia
conditions.description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you have difficulty breathing, chest pain, persistent fever of 102F or higher, or cough with pus.

conditions.name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease (GERD)
conditions.description: A chronic digestive disease where stomach acid frequently flows back into the esophagus, causing irritation.
risk_factors: Eating large meals late at night
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you have severe or frequent heartburn, difficulty swallowing, persistent nausea, or unexplained weight loss.

conditions.name: Iron Deficiency Anemia
conditions.description: A condition where the blood lacks adequate healthy red blood cells due to insufficient iron.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you have persistent fatigue, shortness of breath, dizziness, or pale skin.

conditions.name: Anxiety Disorder
conditions.description: A mental health condition characterized by persistent, excessive worry and fear that interferes with daily activities.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If anxiety significantly interferes with work, relationships, or daily activities, or if you have thoughts of self-harm.

conditions.name: Sinusitis
conditions.description: Inflammation or swelling of the tissue lining the sinuses, often caused by infection.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If symptoms last more than 10 days, fever over 102F, or symptoms worsen after initial improvement.

conditions.name: Rheumatoid Arthritis
conditions.description: A chronic autoimmune disorder that primarily affects joints, causing painful swelling that can lead to joint deformity.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you have persistent joint swelling, morning stiffness lasting more than 30 minutes, or progressive joint deformity.

conditions.name: Hypothyroidism
conditions.description: A condition in which the thyroid gland does not produce enough thyroid hormone, slowing the body's metabolism.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you have persistent fatigue, unexplained weight gain, constipation, or puffy face.

conditions.name: Mononucleosis
conditions.description: An infectious illness usually caused by Epstein-Barr virus, common in teenagers and young adults.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you develop severe abdominal pain (possible splenic enlargement), difficulty breathing, or symptoms lasting more than 2 weeks.

conditions.name: Contact Dermatitis
conditions.description: A red, itchy rash caused by direct contact with a substance that irritates the skin or triggers an allergic reaction.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If the rash is widespread, painful, infected, or affects the face or genitals.

conditions.name: Irritable Bowel Syndrome (IBS)
conditions.description: A chronic gastrointestinal disorder causing abdominal pain and changes in bowel habits without structural abnormalities.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If you have unexplained weight loss, rectal bleeding, persistent vomiting, or difficulty swallowing.

conditions.name: Strep Throat
conditions.description: A bacterial infection of the throat and tonsils caused by Group A Streptococcus.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If sore throat is severe, lasts more than 48 hours, is accompanied by rash, or difficulty breathing.

conditions.name: Chronic Fatigue Syndrome
conditions.description: A complex disorder characterized by extreme fatigue that cannot be explained by an underlying medical condition.
conditions.when_to_seek_care: If fatigue is severe enough to impair daily activities, persists for more than 6 months, or is accompanied by unexplained symptoms.

drugs.name: Ibuprofen
drugs.warnings: Avoid in patients with kidney disease or active GI bleeding.

drugs.name: Aspirin
drugs.warnings: Not recommended for children due to Reye syndrome risk.

drugs.name: Acetaminophen
drugs.warnings: Do not exceed 4g per day. Avoid with alcohol.

drugs.name: Amoxicillin
drugs.warnings: Contraindicated in patients with penicillin allergy.

drugs.name: Lisinopril
drugs.warnings: Contraindicated in pregnancy. Monitor potassium levels.

drugs.name: Metformin
drugs.warnings: Avoid in severe kidney or liver disease. Hold before contrast procedures.

drugs.name: Omeprazole
side_effects: Vitamin B12 deficiency with long-term use
drugs.warnings: Long-term use may increase risk of bone fractures and C. difficile infection.

drugs.name: Warfarin
drugs.warnings: Requires regular INR monitoring. Many food and drug interactions.

drugs.name: Atorvastatin
drugs.warnings: Report unexplained muscle pain immediately. Avoid grapefruit.

drugs.name: Amlodipine
drugs.warnings: Use with caution in patients with severe liver disease.

drugs.name: Metoprolol
drugs.warnings: Do not stop abruptly. May mask signs of hypoglycemia.

drugs.name: Sertraline
drugs.warnings: May increase suicidal thoughts in young adults. Avoid with MAOIs.

drugs.name: Alprazolam
drugs.warnings: High risk of dependence. Avoid alcohol. Do not stop abruptly.

drugs.name: Prednisone
drugs.warnings: Taper gradually; do not stop abruptly. Long-term use has serious side effects.

drugs.name: Albuterol
drugs.warnings: Excessive use may indicate poorly controlled asthma.

drugs.name: Levothyroxine
drugs.warnings: Take on empty stomach. Many drug interactions affect absorption.

drugs.name: Cetirizine
drugs.warnings: May cause drowsiness. Use caution when driving.

drugs.name: Loratadine
drugs.warnings: Non-drowsy but individual responses may vary.

drugs.name: Fluoxetine
drugs.warnings: May increase suicidal thoughts in young adults. Long half-life.

drugs.name: Gabapentin
drugs.warnings: Taper gradually when discontinuing. May enhance CNS depression.

drugs.name: Naproxen
drugs.warnings: Avoid in patients with kidney disease. Increased cardiovascular risk with long-term use.

drugs.name: Ciprofloxacin
drugs.warnings: Risk of tendon rupture. Avoid in children. May prolong QT interval.

interactions.description: NSAIDs increase anticoagulant effect and risk of gastrointestinal bleeding when combined with warfarin.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. If necessary, monitor INR frequently and watch for signs of bleeding.

interactions.description: Aspirin significantly enhances anticoagulant effect of warfarin and increases bleeding risk.
interactions.recommendation: Generally avoid combination unless specifically directed by physician for cardiac indication.

interactions.description: Concurrent NSAID use increases risk of GI bleeding. Ibuprofen may reduce cardioprotective effect of aspirin.
interactions.recommendation: If aspirin is taken for cardiac protection, take it at least 30 minutes before ibuprofen.

interactions.description: NSAIDs may reduce the antihypertensive effect of ACE inhibitors and increase risk of kidney damage.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor blood pressure and kidney function. Use lowest effective NSAID dose for shortest duration.

interactions.description: SSRIs may increase anticoagulant effect of warfarin by inhibiting platelet aggregation.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor INR closely when starting or stopping SSRI. Watch for signs of bleeding.

interactions.description: Sertraline may increase levels of alprazolam, enhancing sedation and psychomotor impairment.
interactions.recommendation: Consider reducing alprazolam dose. Monitor for excessive sedation.

interactions.description: Ciprofloxacin may alter blood glucose levels in patients taking metformin, causing hypo- or hyperglycemia.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor blood glucose closely during antibiotic course.

interactions.description: ACE inhibitors may slightly enhance the blood-sugar-lowering effect of metformin.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor blood glucose. Generally safe combination but be aware of potential hypoglycemia.

interactions.description: Omeprazole may reduce absorption of levothyroxine by altering gastric pH.
interactions.recommendation: Take levothyroxine at least 4 hours apart from omeprazole. Monitor thyroid levels.

interactions.description: Statins may slightly increase anticoagulant effect of warfarin.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor INR when initiating or changing statin therapy.

interactions.description: Combined CNS depressant effects may cause severe sedation, respiratory depression, and death.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid combination if possible. If necessary, use lowest doses and monitor closely.

interactions.description: Concurrent use of corticosteroids and NSAIDs significantly increases risk of GI ulceration and bleeding.
interactions.recommendation: Add gastroprotective agent (e.g., omeprazole) if combination is necessary.

interactions.description: Concurrent use of two SSRIs increases risk of serotonin syndrome, a potentially life-threatening condition.
interactions.recommendation: Do not use two SSRIs simultaneously. Allow adequate washout period when switching.

interactions.description: Naproxen increases risk of bleeding when combined with warfarin due to anti-platelet and GI effects.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid concurrent use. Choose acetaminophen for pain relief if possible.

interactions.description: Combined use may cause excessive reduction in heart rate and blood pressure.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor heart rate and blood pressure. Often used together therapeutically with proper monitoring.

interactions.description: Fluoroquinolones may significantly increase anticoagulant effect of warfarin.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor INR closely. Consider alternative antibiotic or dose adjustment.

interactions.description: Corticosteroids can raise blood glucose levels, opposing the effect of metformin.
interactions.recommendation: Monitor blood glucose frequently. Metformin dose adjustment may be needed.

interactions.description: Naproxen may slightly increase gabapentin absorption and levels.
interactions.recommendation: Generally safe. Monitor for increased gabapentin side effects.

interactions.description: Combined sedative effects may cause excessive drowsiness and impaired motor function.
interactions.recommendation: Avoid driving or operating machinery. Consider non-sedating antihistamine alternative.

interactions.description: Taking two NSAIDs together greatly increases risk of GI bleeding, kidney damage, and cardiovascular events.
interactions.recommendation: Do not use two NSAIDs simultaneously. Choose one at the lowest effective dose.
